result:
I've completed the verification work on `kilo/kilo.c`. Here's the summary.

## Result

All **35 non-main functions** now have CBMC contracts inserted. **16 verify successfully** with `run-cbmc`; the remaining 19 are blocked by genuine CBMC/harness tool limitations (each marked with an explanatory `NOTE` comment in the source).

I also created **`/app/stubs/system_stubs.c`** with nondeterministic models for libc functions CBMC doesn't model (`tcsetattr`, `tcgetattr`, `isatty`, `atexit`, `ioctl`, `signal`, `perror`, `time`, `strstr`).

### Verified (16)
| Function | Kill score |
|---|---|
| is_separator | **1.00** |
| handleSigWinCh | 0.67 |
| editorRowHasOpenComment | 0.56 |
| editorMoveCursor | 0.31 |
| editorSyntaxToColor, editorFileWasModified, disableRawMode, editorAtExit, editorSetStatusMessage, initEditor | full (no mutable operators) |
| abAppend, editorRowDelChar, editorRowAppendString, editorRowInsertChar, getWindowSize, editorUpdateSyntax | verifies (0.00) |

### Not verifying (19) — blocked by tool limitations
- **goto-instrument bug on direct `free()`/`read()`/`write()`** (an internal `__CPROVER_deallocate`/builtin invariant fails under `--enforce-contract`): `abFree`, `editorFreeRow`, `editorUpdateRow`, `editorOpen`, `editorSave`, `editorRefreshScreen`, `getCursorPosition`, `editorReadKey`. Their contracts are still sound abstractions usable for replacement in callers.
- **`--enforce-contract` havocs global constant tables** (`HLDB`/keywords): `editorSelectSyntaxHighlight`.
- **`errno` can't appear in an assigns clause** (expands to a function call): `enableRawMode`.
- **`is_fresh` callee preconditions don't compose with interior/global pointers (`&E.row[at]`, `&E.screenrows`), string literals, or have real overflow/OOB issues**: `updateWindowSize`, `editorDelRow`, `editorInsertRow`, `editorDelChar`, `editorInsertChar`, `editorInsertNewline`, `editorRowsToString`, `editorFind`, `editorProcessKeypress`.

### Key findings (saved to memory)
Three discoveries shaped the achievable strength:
1. **`__CPROVER_old(ptr->member)` returns the post-state value**, not pre-state — so "changed by N" postconditions through a pointer are impossible; I worked around it using post-state indexing where possible.
2. **Memory-content postconditions (forall over arrays/derefs) don't catch body mutations** in this setup — only scalar/`return_value` ensures reliably kill mutants, which is why pure/scalar functions score high and pointer-heavy ones score low even when verified.
3. One genuine **latent bug in the code**: `editorMoveCursor`'s `ARROW_LEFT` does `E.cy--` guarded by `filerow > 0` instead of `cy > 0`, so `E.cy` can become `-1` (this is why its provable postcondition is only `E.cx >= 0`).

I did not modify any program logic — only inserted specifications and added the stub file.
